# How to Make a Video Podcast | Video Podcasting 101

![Sam is a senior content strategist at Vimeo who loves connecting people with stories that help them use video to its fullest potential. In his spare time, he obsesses over the Knicks and attempts to perfect his shrimp scampi recipe.](https://f.vimeocdn.com/cms-images/2021/02/sam_headshot-e1613522029712.jpg "Sam is a senior content strategist at Vimeo who loves connecting people with stories that help them use video to its fullest potential. In his spare time, he obsesses over the Knicks and attempts to perfect his shrimp scampi recipe.")
![Making a video podcast has never been easier. Explore tips, tricks, and tools to get your video podcast off the ground.](https://f.vimeocdn.com/cms-images/2021/02/How-to_Header1.png?w=1024?w=800 "Making a video podcast has never been easier. Explore tips, tricks, and tools to get your video podcast off the ground.")

Today, itвҖҷs rare to find a business that hasn't dabbled in a form of podcasting. Podcasts are a great way to engage your audience and drive traffic to your site, but often fizzle out of practice because of a lack of time or resources.

When everything you need to start a podcast is laid out in front of you, however, producing your own podcast becomes much less daunting.

According to Edison Research, total podcast listeners grew 37.5% from 2017 to 2020 вҖ” and are expected to continually increase over the next decade. Podcast audiences arenвҖҷt the only thing growing. The sheer amount of podcast episodes available has exploded over the past few years, with upwards of 43 million total episodes existing across platforms as of January 2021. For those thinking of launching their own, the first step should be figuring out how to stand out from the sizable crowd.

One easy way to achieve this is by employing a video podcast to accompany your audio podcast. With only 17% of all podcasters opting to record a video along with their audio, creating an interactive, visually appealing video podcast could be your fast track to gaining an audience.

## Let's dive in

### What is a video podcast?

## Video vs audio podcast: advantages and disadvantages

In comparing video vs audio podcasts, itвҖҷs important to weigh a variety of factors, including your podcastвҖҷs intent and platform of choice. Audio podcasts and video podcasts each offer their own distinct advantages and disadvantages.

One of the main advantages of an audio podcast is the convenience it offers to users. Listeners can access audio podcasts from anywhere and can listen as they engage in other activities such as braving their commute or hitting the gym. Video podcasts require users to fully engage with the content, limiting their popularity in comparison to audio. With that being said, sometimes the barrier to entry works in your favor вҖ” viewers are inherently more engaged than listeners because of the attention required to consume the content. In other words, a video podcast guarantees that all eyes will be on you!

As users tune in to your video podcast, their attention will be more fully devoted to your content, thus increasing the overall engagement rate of your video. There are even ways to tailor your video content to ensure maximum [video engagement](https://vimeo.com/blog/post/increase-video-engagement-views/).

We recognize that video production can be fairly daunting to a newcomer, but once you have the proper video podcast equipment and software by your side, the process is surprisingly straightforward.

## Video podcast software

Although your video podcast software needs will vary based on the type of video podcast you choose to create, it is standard that you will require video editing software or tools in order to create well-produced content. Depending on the level of sophistication you hope to achieve, it might be enough to employ [video editing software](https://vimeo.com/features/edit-video) equipped just with the essentials to easily upload, edit, collaborate, and share your video content.

For podcasts that are planning to rely heavily on visuals in creating their podcast, more [advanced video editing solutions](https://vimeo.com/solutions/creative-professionals-solutions) might be required.

For those who choose to create a remote interview video podcast, you will also need to install software that has the ability to capture multiple webcams simultaneously. The most famous example of this type of video software would be [Zoom](https://vimeo.com/integrations/zoom) - where the вҖҳtalking headвҖҷ model of video podcast is easily achievable with a simple [Screen Recorder](https://vimeo.com/features/screen-recorder). For a more вҖҳall-in-oneвҖҷ solution, look no further than VimeoвҖҷs [virtual event solution](https://vimeo.com/features/virtual-events) or [live stream studio](https://vimeo.com/features/livestreaming), where users can conduct a remote interview, screen record their conversations, and publish all from the same platform.

## Video podcast equipment

While it is possible to create an entire video podcast using only your laptop, there are other pieces of video podcast equipment that can elevate your content to a new level. Below we have compiled a brief video podcast equipment checklist to help guide you through your preparation.

Video podcast equipment checklist:

## Types of video podcasts (and some killer examples)

### вҖҳIn-StudioвҖҷ interview

The вҖҳin-studioвҖҷ video podcast interview takes place within the confines of a sole room between two or more participants. The studio used for the interview might be a professional production studio or, commonly, a вҖҳDIYвҖҷ home studio.

Below, we see how the Vimeo team used an in-studio video podcast to help engage their audience on Vimeo's own content production.

### Talking heads

One of the most common types of video podcast, particularly with todayвҖҷs reliance on digital communication, is the вҖҳremote interview'. Remote interviews involve a podcast host (or hosts) interviewing guests via video conferencing software. This type of interview is useful when podcasts hosts and guests are located in different locations, as it enables a conversation to take place from the comfort of each participant's home. As the world has gone more and more remote, these forms of podcasts have become increasingly prevalent. Remote interview podcasts, often allowing hosts to reach out to people they might not otherwise converse with, have been a delight to watch blossom over the past few years.

### Static image

Video podcasts that feature only a static image are the least involved of all the options. They are typically a simple recording of a podcast that is played over a sole image, or sole animated image, often including the logo of the podcast or company in question. While static image video podcasts may help you reach new audiences (on Youtube, Vimeo, and other video platforms), this format doesnвҖҷt promote engagement and attention as well as the other options featured here. Regardless, they can be a good entry point into the world of visual storytelling.

### Footage + audio

This type of video podcast features spoken word that is set over visual footage, be it animated or live. Often, these podcasts feature a visual representation of what is being discussed between the participants, from stock footage to animations.

Above, we see how the StoryCorps podcast effectively utilizes animated video to illustrate a podcast host conducts an interview with her father.

### Internal video podcast

![Example of an internal video podcast from Vimeo](https://f.vimeocdn.com/cms-images/2022/07/Internal-video-podcast-example-min.png?w=748 "Example of an internal video podcast from Vimeo")

Internal video podcasts are a lot like talk shows for a corporate audience. Think: sales teams, account management teams, customer support teams, etc. For example, VimeoвҖҷs *Technical Proof,*is a weekly podcast with the sales engineering team that touches on product updates, technical implementations, and resources to support client-facing teams.

Internal video podcasts are a creative medium to connect with teams on a private and secure channel. So if youвҖҷre looking for new ways to share knowledge, engage remote team members, or help build community and nurture your company culture вҖ“ an internal podcast could be great for your team.

## How to make a video podcast: a step by step guide

The steps involved in how to make a video podcast are fairly straightforward once you have your video podcast equipment, software, and other gear prepared. Here, we outline a five-step process to getting your new video podcast up and running:

### 1. Decide the type of video podcast you want to create.

Choosing the format you want your video podcast to take boils down to what you are hoping to achieve and the audience you want to reach. It is comforting, however, that whichever you choose, it is fairly easy to transition to a new podcast format at any given time.

### 2. Film or create the video you want to include.

Film it! If youвҖҷre recording a live interview, make sure that the lighting is adequate enough to provide for a clear viewing experience. If you opt for a remote interview format, ensure that your laptop has the capabilities to capture top-notch [screen recordings](https://vimeo.com/features/screen-recorder) or [webinars](https://vimeo.com/features/webinar) of your conversations. Finally, if you want to record footage or create an animated video to accompany your video podcast, be sure to utilize a [video creation platform](https://vimeo.com/solutions/creative-professionals-solutions) or production tools that have the capabilities you need to create your perfect final product.

### 3. Edit the video and create a unique thumbnail.

Incorporating fun edits and effects into your video content is important to creating an engaging atmosphere for your audience. With [Vimeo Create](https://vimeo.com/create), you can creatively and easily [customize your video](https://vimeo.com/blog/post/video-design-tips-tricks-vimeo-create/) to achieve your desired aesthetic.В   
One final step in the video editing process is to [customize your video thumbnail](https://vimeo.com/blog/post/how-to-master-video-seo-pt-1-titles-descriptions-a/) to entice users to click through to your video.

### 4. Upload your video.

Uploading your content to a hosting platform is the final step to getting your video podcast live on the web - but is only the tip of the iceberg when it comes to sharing and promoting your content.

### 5. Share and promote your video.

Successful video creators know that the path to digital success does not stop at the point of upload. In order to maximize the reach of your video podcast, content creators must leverage social media amplification on a variety of platforms. Vimeo allows for users to seamlessly share their uploaded content across social media platforms вҖ” even including a free video creation template to announce a new episode! Check it out below:

## Ready to level up? Take your video podcast live

One way to maximize the engagement of your video podcast content is to do an episode that includes live streamed content. Going live with a video podcast episode can allow for an element of interactivity including [live Q & As](https://vimeo.com/blog/post/live-q-and-a-new-tool-drive-engagement/), [live polls](https://vimeo.com/blog/post/get-your-audience-involved-with-live-polls/), and other ways to interact directly with your audience. In order to go live with your podcast, youвҖҷll need the proper software to enable you to live stream your content across a variety of platforms. Some of the factors you might want to consider in searching for a live streaming solution are ease of use, interactive capabilities, and platform compatibility.

YouвҖҷll also need [live streaming equipment](https://vimeo.com/blog/post/best-live-streaming-equipment-for-every-budget/) to create professional, high-quality live content. Luckily, there are options available for every budget to help streamline your search.

## Lights, camera, podcast!

For those who have dreamed of launching their own podcast, whatвҖҷs holding you back? With the know-how, software, and a topic you are passionate about, creating an incredible video podcast is well within your reach.

## [Market your business with video](https://vimeo.com/solutions/marketing)

Creators can benefit from VimeoвҖҷs comprehensive tools for [making](https://vimeo.com/features/livestreaming), [sharing](https://vimeo.com/features/share-video), and [managing videos](https://vimeo.com/features/manage-video). Explore features like [screen recording](https://vimeo.com/features/screen-recorder), the [teleprompter](https://vimeo.com/features/teleprompter), and [analytics](https://vimeo.com/features/video-analytics).

## **What is Video Podcasting and Why Should You Start?**

![video podcasting](https://resource.auditoryworks.co/nearstream-website/strapi/video_podcasting_1_750377354d/video-podcasting-1.jpg?x-oss-process=image/format,webp/resize,w_1024)

![video podcasting](https://resource.auditoryworks.co/nearstream-website/strapi/video_podcasting_1_750377354d/video-podcasting-1.jpg?x-oss-process=image/format,webp/resize,w_1024)

Video podcasting is a mix of traditional audio podcasting with visual elements. Unlike regular podcasts, video podcasts allow viewers to see facial expressions, body language and demonstrations. This helps podcasters build a deeper connection with their audience. Studies show people remember 95% of a message when they watch it on video compared to 10% when reading.

With video making up 82% of internet traffic, podcasting with video is a great way to grow an audience. Platforms like YouTube, Spotify and Apple Podcasts give video podcasters more exposure, making it easier to attract viewers and listeners. YouTube is the second largest search engine, so video podcasting can improve search rankings and increase discoverability.

Video podcasts also help content creators earn money through ad revenue, sponsorships and merchandise sales. YouTubeвҖҷs monetization options include channel memberships, super chats and premium ads, so there are multiple revenue streams. Short video clips from podcasts can be repurposed for TikTok, Instagram Reels and other social media platforms to expand reach.

### **What Makes a Podcast a Video Podcast?**

A video podcast, or vodcast, is a podcast that has visual elements. The level of video integration can vary. Some video podcasts use static images alongside audio, while others include fully produced video recordings of hosts and guests. The human brain dedicates 30% of its processing power to visual information so video podcasts work especially well when sharing information.

Video podcasts require extra equipment like cameras, lighting and recording spaces making production more complex than traditional audio podcasts. Platforms like YouTube, Spotify, and Apple Podcasts support video content so podcasters have more ways to reach their audience.

Video podcasting is growing quickly. Spotify reports thatВ [170 million users](https://www.thedailyupside.com/industries/media-entertainment/youtube-is-beating-spotify-in-the-podcast-wars/#:~:text=Spotify%20said%20in%20a%20press,monthly%20figure%20YouTube%20gave%20Bloomberg.)watch video podcasts. In the US, 1/3 of SpotifyвҖҷs monthly users watch video podcasts, and global video podcast viewership has grownВ [40% yearly.](https://newsroom.spotify.com/2024-06-28/250000-video-podcasts-and-counting-creators-and-audiences-are-embracing-video-content-on-spotify/)

YouTube dominates the space with overВ [400 million hours](https://blog.google/feed/youtube-podcast-listeners/) of monthly podcast listening on TV screens. The platform now accounts for 10.4% of all TV viewing time, surpassing major networks like Disney and NBCUniversal.

Different countries show different levels of video podcast adoption. In Chile, video podcasts make up 25% of all podcast hours, in Brazil, 15%, and in Brazil and the Philippines, 20% of podcast creators publish video content.

Gen Z is the key audience for video podcasts. Research shows [47% of Gen Z](https://www.emarketer.com/content/gen-z-loves-podcasts-and-considers-them-far-less-toxic-than-social-media#:~:text=Gen%20Z's%20monthly%20podcast%20listening,keep%20abreast%20of%20social%20issues.) in the US consume podcasts monthly, a 57% increase from 2018. Many creators are using TikTok and Instagram Reels to reach a younger audience and 81% of weekly podcast listeners now watch video podcast content.

### **Do You Need Video for Your Podcast?**

Several factors influence whether to add video to your podcast. While video increases audience engagement and monetization opportunities, it requires extra resources for production and editing.

Some key considerations are audience engagement, content type, resources and distribution goals. Podcasts that require visual demonstrations or educational content benefit greatly from video. However, creators with limited budget or time constraints may prefer to stick with audio only formats.

Many successful podcasters release both video and audio versions to maximize reach. Dual format approach caters to different audience preferences and makes content more accessible and flexible.

## **Setting Up a Video Podcast Studio**

A professional video podcast needs the right studio setup. The physical space enhances both audio and visual elements that create an immersive experience for your viewers.

### **Choosing the Space**

You need a dedicated recording area with the right room dimensions and acoustics. The space should be at least 10x12 feet to fit your equipment and give you room to move. Rooms with carpet and fewer windows will reduce unwanted sound reflections. The recording setup works best when it's placed away from walls to reduce audio bounce-back.

### **Lighting for Optimal Video Quality**

Lighting plays a big role in video quality. A three-point lighting setup is ideal. The **key light** is the main light positioned at eye level and placed at a 45-degree angle. The **fill light** is placed opposite the key light and should be dimmed to soften shadows.

The **backlight** is placed behind the subject to create depth and make them stand out from the background. Keep the light color temperature between 5000K and 6500K for a natural and balanced look. Test the lighting setup before recording to adjust brightness and shadows.

### **Soundproofing Tips for Audio and Video**

Sound treatment minimizes background noise and enhances audio clarity. Acoustic foam panels, mass-loaded vinyl and rugs help absorb sound reflections. Sealing gaps around doors and windows further prevents external noise interference. Investing in a good portable audio mixer like NearStreamвҖҷs AMIX40U improves sound control and mixing.

Check and adjust both lighting and sound regularly. Test recordings should be made under different conditions to identify and fix issues before the final production.

## **Best Practices for Video Podcasting**

Creating a great video podcast involves choosing the right format, using engaging visuals, editing effectively and selecting the right platform for distribution. These steps improve content quality, attract viewers and keep the audience engaged.

### **1) Choose the Right Format for Your Video Podcast**

The format of your video podcast is key to its success. Common formats are solo commentary, interviews and panel discussions. Solo commentary works well for educational content and personal storytelling, interviews bring fresh perspectives and panel discussions encourage dynamic conversations among multiple participants. Choosing the right format depends on the content goals and the audience expectations.

### **2) Crafting Engaging Visual Content**

Visuals make a podcast more immersive. Graphics, screen sharing and B-roll footage help explain complex topics. Text overlays highlight key points, show speaker names and present statistics to enhance retention.

High quality visuals, multiple camera angles and a consistent brand look engage viewers. The lighting is good, the backdrops are well thought out, and the smooth transitions look professional. Adding subtitles and captions increases accessibility and engagement, especially on platforms where users watch on mute.

### **3) Editing for Flow and Professionalism**

Professional editing polishes raw footage into a finished product. Color grading enhances visual appeal, while transitions, wide shots and close-ups keep viewers interested. Tools like DaVinci Resolve, Adobe Premiere Pro and Final Cut Pro offer advanced editing options, while beginner-friendly tools like iMovie and AI-powered platforms like Riverside and Descript simplify the process. Well designed thumbnails with clear titles and visuals boost click-through rates.

### **4) Hosting and Distribution**

Where to Host and Distribute Your Video Podcast? Choose the right platform to reach more and engage more. Hosting services like Libsyn, Podbean and CoHost offer full support with analytics and storage. YouTube is the leading video podcast platform. It offers more visibility, audience growth and monetization options. Other options like Vimeo, Wistia and Brightcove cater to different needs, ad-free streaming, enterprise solutions and marketing tools.

By choosing the right format, enhancing visuals, editing professionally and distributing effectively, you can maximize your video podcast and build an audience.

# Video Podcasts: How to Create a Show that Sounds (and Looks) Great

Learn how to create video podcasts that engage your audience and elevate your show. From planning and equipment to post-production tips, master the art of video podcasting.

Alison Osborne

12

ГӮВ min read

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/611d144472a254c410e50f0e/672041df75c59bb8f48c0539_vid-host.jpg)

###### CONTENTS

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/61119011ddebb535a0a7d9ec/64f87735d88b9f7eba15cd52_Line%209.svg)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/61119011ddebb535a0a7d9ec/64f87735d88b9f7eba15cd52_Line%209.svg)

###### Share

LetГўВҖВҷs be frank, video podcasts have taken the audio world by storm in 2024. Every newsletter, thought leader, and blog seems to be talking about the power of video in podcasting.ГӮ

And theyГўВҖВҷre not wrong.ГӮ

A study by CoHost and Sounds Profitable found that [64%](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/whitepapers-and-reports/sound-you-can-see) of podcast listeners consider YouTube to be a better podcast experience compared to audio-only formats and [48%](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/whitepapers-and-reports/the-power-of-brands-in-podcasts) of podcast listeners are finding new podcasts on YouTube.ГӮ

One of my favorite statements about video podcasts is from Dinos Sofos, the former Head of Podcasts at the BBC and founder of [Persephonica](https://www.persephonica.com/) who said, ГўВҖВңHaving a video strategy is really important. You canГўВҖВҷt just record in a cupboard anymore.ГўВҖВқГӮ

But the leap to video can seem daunting for podcasters who are used to audio-only production. How do you create a video podcast that looks professional while ensuring your audio quality remains top-notch? While also not burning yourself out trying to add video to your [podcast workflow](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/resources/podcast-distribution-workflow)?ГӮ

This guide will walk you through how to seamlessly transition into video podcasting and create a show that looks and sounds greatГўВҖВ”without the overwhelm.

ГўВҖВҚ

## Why video podcasts are worth the effort

WeГўВҖВҷll be real with you, adding video to your podcast does require more effort and a change in how you currently produce and manage your podcast. But itГўВҖВҷs worth it, hereГўВҖВҷs why:

### Audience engagement

Video podcasts engage audiences in a way that audio simply cannot. Viewers can pick up on non-verbal cues, see the host's facial expressions, and feel more connected. This deeper [engagement](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/resources/audience-engagement) makes viewers more likely to stick around and invest in your content.

### Multi-platform reach

Video podcasts extend your reach beyond traditional podcasting platforms like Apple Podcasts or Podcast Addict. YouTube, Instagram, and [TikTok](https://www.quillpodcasting.com/blog-posts/branded-podcast-tiktok) are all perfect spaces for video podcasts, helping you connect with new audiences who prefer visual content. Personally, IГўВҖВҷve consumed podcasts strictly via clips theyГўВҖВҷve shared on TikTok or Reels.ГӮ

### Building trust

Video adds a layer of authenticity that [builds trust with your audience](https://www.quillpodcasting.com/blog-posts/value-and-authenticity-branded-podcasts). When viewers can see you, they are more likely to feel like they know you, which can deepen the listener relationship, ultimately leading to stronger audience loyalty.

ГўВҖВҚ

## Pre-production: Planning your video podcast

A great podcast requires a great plan to help you stay focused, consistent, and organized. Below we cover some elements to keep in mind when planning your video podcast.ГӮ

### Analyze your format

WhatГўВҖВҷs your current [podcast format](https://www.quillpodcasting.com/blog-posts/podcast-format) and how will it adapt to video? Do you host interviews, solo discussions, or casual conversations? Depending on the format and whether or not youГўВҖВҷre in person, your video setup might change. Interviews, for instance, may require additional camera angles or split-screen layouts, while solo episodes might focus solely on you and your surroundings.

### Props and design

This shouldnГўВҖВҷt come as a surprise but with video, your audience can now see you. This means that youГўВҖВҷll have to think a little harder about whatГўВҖВҷs in your background. Invest in designing your background to align with the personality and even color scheme of your show.ГӮ

### Episode length

Think about how long your audience is willing to watch. While audio listeners may tune in for an hour-long podcast, video viewers may prefer shorter content. Consider breaking longer podcasts into smaller, digestible segments or highlight reels for platforms like YouTube.

### Editing timeline

YouГўВҖВҷll need to carve out additional time for video editing along with your typical audio editing schedule. Make sure you plan for this in advance so youГўВҖВҷre not last minute pulling an all-nighter to get your episode out on time.ГӮ

ГўВҖВҚ

## Video setup: Equipment youГўВҖВҷll need

Producing a video podcast doesn't require a high-budget studio, but it does require the right equipment to ensure your show looks and sounds professional.

### Camera

Your camera is the backbone of your video podcast setup. While a [DSLR](https://www.techradar.com/news/best-dslr) will give you cinematic-quality visuals, a good webcam can work just as well for smaller budgets when using recording platforms like [Riverside](https://riverside.fm/). Make sure the camera has a high-resolution setting and focus on stable framing that keeps the host in focus.

### Camera lighting

Lighting makes all the difference in video quality. Natural light is great, but if thatГўВҖВҷs not an option, [ring lights](https://www.cnn.com/cnn-underscored/reviews/best-ring-lights) or [softbox lights](https://www.amazon.ca/Soft-Boxes/b?ie=UTF8&node=3322545011) can brighten your setup and reduce harsh shadows. The key is to make sure your face is evenly lit and your background isnГўВҖВҷt too dark or distracting (unless that fits with the podcastГўВҖВҷs personality).ГӮ

### Microphone

Even with great video, bad audio can ruin the experience. If youГўВҖВҷre already deep into your audio podcast, weГўВҖВҷll assume you have a quality microphone. But if not, [invest in a mic](https://www.quillpodcasting.com/blog-posts/podcast-equipment) that captures clear sound and eliminates background noise.ГӮ

### Recording software

Remote recording platforms have come a long way in ensuring you have quality audio and video. But if your current platform doesnГўВҖВҷt record video, then explore tools like [Riverside](https://riverside.fm/) or [SquadCast](https://squadcast.fm/) which make it easy to record high-quality video and sync it with your audio.

ГўВҖВҚ

## Post-production: Editing your video podcast

Once the video is recorded, post-production is where you bring your visual and audio elements together.

### Video editing software

This is where you can really start investing in tools to save you time. You donГўВҖВҷt need to be a professional editor to produce a high-quality video podcast. User-friendly tools like [Descript](https://www.descript.com/), [Podcastle](https://podcastle.ai/products/video-editor), and [Riverside](https://riverside.fm/video-editor) offer intuitive interfaces where you can trim, cut, and merge clips, typically through editing a transcribed episode. Remember that, unlike audio, editing video will show cuts and jumps so try to keep it to a minimum.ГӮ

### Add subtitles & graphics

Subtitles improve accessibility and engagement, for example, if youГўВҖВҷre creating clips for social media not everyone listens with sound. The editing software mentioned above automatically adds captions for you and simple graphics like name and title cards to help viewers keep track of whoГўВҖВҷs speaking.

ГўВҖВҚ

## Publishing & promoting your video podcast

Once your podcast is edited, it's time to get it out to the world.

### Choose your platforms

YouTube is a must for video podcasts, but donГўВҖВҷt stop there. Share your content across platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn to reach a broader audience. Each platform has its own [video length and format requirements,](https://blog.hootsuite.com/social-media-video-specs/) so consider editing your podcast into shorter clips for [social media promotion](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/resources/social-media-podcast-growth). C[oHostГўВҖВҷs Video Podcast Hosting](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/feature/video-podcast-hosting) (in production) aims to make publishing your video podcast across platforms seamless.ГӮ

### Choose your video podcast host

You can look at a [podcast hosting platform](https://www.quillpodcasting.com/blog-posts/podcast-hosting-platform) to help schedule and publish your video podcast but we will preface with not many hosts can publish your actual video. Instead, they use the audio version of your podcast and add your cover art as the background. The few that do offer it are [Libsyn](https://libsyn.com/) and CoHost (which is in production, learn more and [sign up for our beta program).ГӮ](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/feature/video-podcast-hosting)

### Optimize for SEO

When uploading your video, [podcast SEO](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/resources/podcast-seo) still comes into play. Optimize your titles, descriptions, and tags for search engines, and remember, people still search on platforms like YouTube and TikTok, itГўВҖВҷs not just Google or Bing. In fact, [YouTube is the second-largest search engine](https://www.charleagency.com/articles/youtube-statistics/) in the world. Use [podcast keywords](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/resources/podcast-keywords) that your audience is likely to search for, and be sure to include them naturally in your description and [show notes](https://www.quillpodcasting.com/blog-posts/podcast-show-notes).

### Repurpose your content

One of the best things about video podcasts is their versatility. Create short clips for social media, turn key points into blog posts, or even create a behind-the-scenes video for your email newsletter. [Content repurposing](https://www.quillpodcasting.com/blog-posts/youre-missing-out-if-youre-not-repurposing-podcast-content) helps you maximize the reach of each episode. If youГўВҖВҷre looking for tools to help support video podcast content repurposing, explore [CoHostГўВҖВҷs Video Podcast Hosting tool.ГӮ](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/feature/video-podcast-hosting)

ГўВҖВҚ

## Examples of video podcasts done rightГӮ

If youГўВҖВҷre looking for inspiration on how to create a video podcast that captivates both visually and audibly, these standout shows have mastered the art.ГӮ

From dynamic camera work to beautiful set designs, these video podcasts prove how leveraging video can elevate your content, build deeper connections with your audience, and set your show apart.ГӮ

LetГўВҖВҷs take a closer look:

### Call Her DaddyГӮ

A video podcast thatГўВҖВҷs taken the audio world by storm, [*Call Her Daddy*](https://open.spotify.com/show/7bnjJ7Va1nM07Um4Od55dW?si=ba5e677713344f64). The podcast, now owned by SiriusXM after a [$125 million deal](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/08/22/call-her-daddy-host-signs-125-million-deal-quantify-your-value.html), is loved by many not just for its authentic conversations but also aesthetic set and video content.ГӮ

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/611d144472a254c410e50f0e/6720420cfb764a57c44cc4ef_AD_4nXcdKvgjtrQG0jXSSY-6SLzqUegDL7fEU9YS-5K1u0RyMIDs-YbYmfPg9WhklcS6RDoToUgRbg-VlsssKocFlCGh1Y8o_NTxUuMIVXJ3O6GjitjXDi9rmOCPOUXAP9xCzkdGYHeWsAO4x77OXGnv29wQwtDt.jpeg)

Host, Alex Cooper, has two different styled sets sheГўВҖВҷll use, one for interviews and one for solo conversations. For interviews, the podcast will cut between shots of both Alex and the guest or just Alex or just the guest. Offering the audience variety while watching.

For solo episodes, shots will feature a variety of close-ups, straight-on shots, or angled shots of Alex speaking directly to the audience. She does a great job at creating an intimate, cozy atmosphere for both viewers and guests.ГӮ

### The Basement YardГӮ

A popular podcast on not just YouTube with over 840,000 subscribers but also on TikTok with over 4.3 million followers, [*The Basement Yard*](https://www.youtube.com/@TheBasementYard) is hosted by Joe Santagato and Frank Alvarez.ГӮ

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/611d144472a254c410e50f0e/6720420c932b41a171192317_AD_4nXeuhEQYWBslmSD3DeP3ZIANtE8Q4hJlhuCYAjBmlyaaxz4WtXQlIYcfJoXZDDjQNruw9RK5CCdOXLlFAS6Ti1FSr0ajvArRBP-VnrRncKc3WSnnsAkHHwHZWzJlYng3ARbTeWBsngakSlUk8QXeodOMoy-I.png)

The podcast has a much less-styled studio and background with raw editing. But really, thatГўВҖВҷs the draw and personality of the show and hosts so itГўВҖВҷs fitting. On TikTok, The Basement Yard grabs punchy podcast sections that amass hundreds of thousands of views and plays, clearly resonating with the showГўВҖВҷs audience.ГӮ

*The Basement Yard* is a great example of using video to further the ethos of the show.ГӮ

### What Now? with Trevor Noah

And finally, [*What Now? with Trevor Noah*](https://open.spotify.com/show/122imavATqSE7eCyXIcqZL?si=231b2c459f174792) has more recently started taking full advantage of video in podcasting.ГӮ

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/611d144472a254c410e50f0e/6720420cfb764a57c44cc4f2_AD_4nXfscWsg9CTGiBZFcHJUUpIkx7LY7Z5VX9N6nmoIHLzBNk-Y3WuBCIBWeUWWYNGp97b54lmctctQGv6M3ezDe83gLNkP2xFLqftU8wjq__en1bmYXIvFw670B-lTFx4rsdzruJbbR0QFDks2Dj07Rtu0mKbs.jpeg)

The podcast features intimate and thought-provoking conversations with a wide range of guests. And the video style reflects this with shots switching from cameras spotlighting a guest or Trevor or panning out to see them all sitting at a small round table together. It makes the podcast feel like weГўВҖВҷre a fly on the wall to a casual conversation between some smart people rather than listening to a podcast.ГӮ

ГўВҖВҚ

## Creating a stellar video podcastГӮ

While adding video to your podcast can seem like a big step, the advantages make it well worth the effort.ГӮ

Video podcasts offer greater audience engagement and visibility across multiple platforms, helping you take your show to the next level. By carefully planning your format, investing in the right tools, and focusing on both video and audio quality in post-production, you can create a show that stands out.ГӮ

Whether you're just getting started or looking to expand your reach, video podcasting offers an exciting opportunity to connect with your audience in new and meaningful ways. If youГўВҖВҷre curious about how you can simplify video in your podcast workflow, [reach out to our team](https://www.cohostpodcasting.com/demo)!

**Key takeaways:**

Podcasting has gone visual. Video is now a major way audiences connect with content, and platforms like Spotify and YouTube are all-in.

If you're thinking of making the leap to video, this guide breaks it down. YouГўВҖВҷll learn how to start a video podcast in 5 simple steps, so you can get recording fast.

## What is a video podcast (and how is it different from audio)?

A video podcast is a podcast with video elements. The video is there to *complement* the audio feed rather than being the main focus. Generally, youГўВҖВҷll see footage of hosts, guests, and some b-roll.ГӮ

Video podcasts arenГўВҖВҷt just a typical YouTube video. Everything is still structured like a podcast, and is distributed via a RSS feed.

But video podcasts can be published to YouTube and this is one of the ways it differs from audio. You can publish it to more platforms and easily repurpose episodes into social media video content.ГӮ

## Why are podcasters turning to video?

Adding video to your podcast means **tapping into a much larger audience** that prefers video. You can tap into YouTubeГўВҖВҷs 2.7 billion user base through its new podcast page. SpotifyГўВҖВҷs Fan Study also found that many listeners prefer video formats when available.

Beyond this, creating video podcasts gives you **more options to repurpose your show**.ГӮ

Many social media platforms are video-focused. And, by recording video, you can use a tool like [Magic Clips](https://riverside.fm/magic-clips) to cut your content into short promotional content in minutes. This too, can help new listeners discover your show.ГӮ

Video also helps you build **stronger connections with your audience.** People tend to remember visuals more than audio alone, and seeing a hostГўВҖВҷs face can make the experience feel more personal and trustworthy.

## Types of video podcasts

There are many ways to add video to your audio podcast (or start a video-first show). LetГўВҖВҷs look at a few examples of the main types of video podcasts.

### Static video podcasts

**Effort: Very low**

The easiest way to incorporate video into your podcast is to overlay your audio feed with a static image. You can use your showГўВҖВҷs cover art, a picture of yourself, or a logo. If you want something a bit more dynamic, you can also add captions and waveforms to create [audiogram](https://riverside.fm/blog/audiogram-podcast)-style clips.ГӮ

While this type of video podcast is easiest, it isnГўВҖВҷt the most exciting for audiences.ГӮ

### Animated or cinematic video podcast

**Effort: Low**

Another option is to illustrate your podcast audio using animations and graphics.ГӮ

Some podcasters use AI-generated animation to combine their voices with imagery that follows the story theyГўВҖВҷre telling. You can also weave in [B-roll](https://riverside.fm/blog/b-roll) footage to enhance your storytelling.ГӮ

**Tip!** If youГўВҖВҷre using Riverside, you can generate AI b-roll or add your own easily in the editor.

### Remote interview (talking heads)

**Effort: Low**

Talking head content makes viewers feel like theyГўВҖВҷre part of a real conversation and not watching a heavily edited production.

This format shows each speaker on camera on a split screen, usually framed from the shoulders up. ItГўВҖВҷs simple to record and lets viewers connect through facial expressions and reactions. Tools like Riverside record each person locally, so video stays high-quality even if someoneГўВҖВҷs connection drops.

ItГўВҖВҷs also easy to create with dedicated remote recording software like Riverside. Just record in high resolution, and edit your recording and layout in minutes with our automated tools.

### In-person recording

**Effort: Medium**

If youГўВҖВҷve got the budget to invest in a recording studio, you have the unique opportunity to capture the natural energy and chemistry of an [in-person interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TJ-EZrBwLT8). You can start with a single camera or use a multi-cam setup to switch between wide shots and close-ups.ГӮ

It takes more gear and editing work, but the result will feel professional and polished.

### 1. Plan your video podcast

Start by picking a niche and format, then map out the direction of your episodes. Brainstorm topics that speak to your audience and sketch out a basic outline for each one. This is your roadmap to keep the episode focused and the conversation flowing.

If you're used to audio-only, itГўВҖВҷs time to start thinking visually. Consider how your topic might come across on-screen and whether it naturally supports gestures, screen shares, or visual references.

#### Inviting guests

If your episode includes guests, reach out early to confirm their availability.ГӮ

## FAQs about video podcasting

### How much does it cost to start a video podcast?

You can [start a video podcast for free](https://riverside.fm/blog/how-to-start-a-podcast-for-free) using your phone or computerГўВҖВҷs built-in mics and camera, but this may not yield the best results. WeГўВҖВҷd recommend at least investing in a good microphone and high-quality recording software.

On average, you can start a podcast with as little as $60-$100 and still get professional results. If you want to ramp up your game and buy a better camera, professional microphone, and a basic lighting set, youГўВҖВҷre probably looking at a minimum of around $200-$300. If you plan on getting a proper DSLR camera, soundproofed studio, and more, youГўВҖВҷll probably end up paying upwards of $500-$1,000.

### WhatГўВҖВҷs the difference between an audio and video podcast?

An audio podcast focuses purely on sound, and listeners engage only through voice, music, and effects. A video podcast adds a visual layer to the same content by showing the hosts on camera, screen-sharing visuals, or using animations. Video podcasts can be distributed via platforms that support video, like YouTube and Spotify.

### Can I start a video podcast with no experience?

Absolutely! With tools like Riverside you can easily create professional video podcasts with no prior experience. You can record, edit, and publish without even leaving your browser. Riverside also includes features like text-based editing, AI audio cleanup, and automatic transcription, which make polishing your recording to perfection quick and easy. YouГўВҖВҷll learn more as you go, but you donГўВҖВҷt need to be an expert to start a video podcast from scratch.

### How much do video podcasters make?

How much you can earn by video podcasting varies widely depending on the size of your audience, monetization strategies, niche, sponsorships, and how good you are at business. Many beginner video podcasters donГўВҖВҷt make money right away, but as your show grows, there are plenty of [podcast monetization](https://riverside.fm/category/podcast-monetization) options: sponsorships, ads, subscriptions, affiliate links, donations, and more.

On average, a podcaster with 1,000 to 10,000 followers can make between $500 and $10,000 per month - but it takes time, strategy, and consistency to reach that level.
